fahim_mougamadou.name: Fahim Mougamadou
fahim_mougamadou.summary:
A North Carolina competitor, Fahim Mougamadou is steadily building a competitive resume, currently competing at the U15 level, with 11 entries across 5 tournaments. Every tournament adds experience, every match sharpens the game.

What makes Fahim especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines. Competing across all disciplines builds a well-rounded game that pure specialists can't replicate.

In doubles, Fahim has formed effective partnerships:
  • Adithya S Burisetty (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in BD (5 events, best: #5)

Fahim has competed at both U13 and U15, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

With tournaments spanning Midwest, NE, South, Fahim isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

faith_yan.name: Faith Yan
faith_yan.summary:
Hailing from New Jersey, Faith Yan is a consistent semifinalist, currently competing at the U13 level, reaching the final four 4 times. That kind of reliability at the top of the draw is the signature of a player who will soon be contending for titles.

Faith is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting a singles best of #3, a doubles best of #3. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Faith has built chemistry with several doubles partners:
  • Junqi Wan (current partner) — currently teaming up in XD (2 events, best: #5)
  • Arushi Saha (current partner) — currently teaming up in GD (2 events, best: #3)

feiyu_wu.summary:
Feiyu Wu has shown real competitive teeth, currently competing at the U19 level, breaking into the semifinals 1 time. Across 2 entries in 1 tournaments, every deep run is a signal of growing strength.

Feiyu competes in both singles and doubles, developing the kind of well-rounded game that pays dividends as competition intensifies at higher levels.

felicia_ho.name: Felicia Ho
felicia_ho.summary:
Hailing from California, Felicia Ho has reached the podium at the ORC level (top-3), currently competing at the U17 level, competing against the best players in the region. Open Regional Championships are a significant step up from local events, and Felicia's results show the readiness for top-tier competition.

Notably, Felicia has entered higher age groups at 2 tournaments, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

Felicia is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting a singles best of #2, a doubles best of #2, a mixed doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event participant (3 entries) • Junior Nationals competitor (6 entries) • ORC top-3 finisher.

Felicia has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 27 different players across the career:
  • Daniel Bicheng Fong (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in XD (5 events, across 2 seasons)
  • Maximus Nguyen (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in XD (4 events, best: #3)
  • Shivani Ramakrishnan (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in GD (4 events, best: #3, spanning 3 seasons)
  • Celine Ao (current partner) — currently teaming up in GD (2 events, across 2 seasons)

A veteran of the junior circuit, Felicia has competed across U11, U13, U15 and U17, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Felicia has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 5 seeded entries across the career, Felicia consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience, Felicia brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

2024-2025.t: 2025 YONEX Dave Freeman Jr. SoCal OLC

Felix competes in both singles and mixed doubles, developing the kind of well-rounded game that pays dividends as competition intensifies at higher levels.

felix_zhang.name: Felix Zhang
felix_zhang.summary:
A Missouri competitor, Felix Zhang has claimed a championship, currently competing at the U19 level, a moment that separates contenders from champions. With 20 entries across 8 tournaments, the competitive foundation is built — and more titles are within reach.

Felix is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (3 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

A true triple-threat, Felix competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 1 doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (3 entries).

Felix has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 5 different players across the career:
  • Vedaant Sreedharan (current primary partner) — 3 events together this season alone with 1 title, building serious momentum (7 total events in BD, spanning 5 seasons)

A veteran of the junior circuit, Felix has competed across U15, U17 and U19, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Felix has competed across 4 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience and 1 title, Felix is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

fiona_liu.name: Fiona Liu
fiona_liu.summary:
Hailing from Texas, Fiona Liu is a multi-time champion on the junior circuit, currently competing at the U13 level, capturing 3 titles across 18 tournaments and 48 entries. Each win is earned through bracket battles against hungry opponents — and Fiona keeps finding ways to come out on top.

Fiona is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting 2 singles titles, a doubles best of #2, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (3 entries).

With 17 different doubles partners over the years, Fiona adapts to anyone:
  • Allen Wang (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in XD (9 events, best: #3, spanning 3 seasons)
  • Xirui Wang (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in GD (3 events, best: #2, across 2 seasons)
  • David Zhang (current partner) — currently teaming up in XD (2 events, across 2 seasons)
  • Kyle Chen (current partner) — currently teaming up in XD (2 events, best: #5)

Fiona is peaking at the right time in 2025-2026, with 1 title across 7 tournaments — a clear sign that Fiona is still ascending.

Fiona has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Fiona has competed across 5 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

francesca_corbett.name: Francesca Corbett
francesca_corbett.summary:
A California competitor, Francesca Corbett has reached the pinnacle of American junior badminton — selected for the U.S. Junior National Team. Winning at the Selection Event (U19 GD) is the ultimate validation: it means being chosen to represent the country on the international stage. With 4 career titles across 3 tournaments, this is a player who delivers when everything is on the line.

Notably, Francesca has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

A true triple-threat, Francesca competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting a singles best of #3, 1 doubles title, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event champion (U19 GD) — selected to represent the USA • Junior Nationals champion (GD U19, XD U17).

Francesca has built chemistry with several doubles partners:
  • Allison Q Lee (current partner) — an ongoing partnership that keeps producing results: 3 titles across 3 events in GD/U19, spanning 3 seasons
  • [] (current partner) — currently teaming up in U19 (2 events, across 2 seasons)
  • Samuel Wales Li — 1 events, 1 title together in XD

Francesca has competed at both U17 and U19, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience, Francesca brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

franklin_xu_cheng.name: Franklin Xu Cheng
franklin_xu_cheng.summary:
Hailing from Texas, Franklin Xu Cheng is a Regional Championship titlist, currently competing at the U13 level, proving the ability to triumph against the strongest regional fields. Open Regional Championships draw the top talent from across the region, and taking home the title is a genuine achievement.

Notably, Franklin has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

A true triple-threat, Franklin competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting a singles best of #2, 2 doubles titles, a mixed doubles best of #3. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (3 entries) • 2 ORC titles.

Diving into the match data reveals: Franklin boasts 51 straight-games victories, showing the ability to close out matches decisively, a biggest win margin of 36 points, a clutch 56% win rate in three-game matches (9/16).

Franklin has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 18 different players across the career:
  • Emily Feng (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in XD (7 events, best: #3, across 2 seasons)
  • Adrian Zhang (current primary partner) — 6 events together this season alone with 2 titles, building serious momentum (6 total events in BD, across 2 seasons)

The 2025-2026 season has been outstanding, with 7 podium finishes across 7 tournaments. Franklin is playing some of the best badminton of the career right now.

Franklin has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Franklin has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience, Franklin brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.
